title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Stress
intro: Herbal teas for stress are a type of herbal remedy that utilizes plant extracts to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, tension, and emotional distress. These teas can be effective in treating stress due to their calming properties, which are often attributed to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. Some examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat stress include chamomile tea, which is known for its soothing effects and ability to promote relaxation; lavender tea, which has been shown to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality; passionflower tea, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce restlessness; peppermint tea, which can aid in digestion and reduce feelings of overwhelm; and valerian root tea, which is often used as a natural sedative to promote relaxation and reduce stress. By incorporating these teas into one's daily routine, individuals can experience a decrease in stress levels and an improvement in overall well-being.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat stress and anxiety due to their sedative and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat stress by promoting a calm and peaceful state of mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and restlessness, and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis teas, including valerenic acid, valeranone, and valepotriates, are responsible for its anxiolytic and sedative effects, which help to alleviate stress and promote relaxation. Regular consumption of Valeriana officinalis teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including reduced stress and anxiety levels, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root per cup of boiling water. If you prefer a stronger tea, use 2 teaspoons.
Steep the Valeriana officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the root.
Drink the tea as needed to help with stress relief. You can drink it up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Valeriana officinalis teas for stress show to significantly attenuate the subjective emotional stress response during an acute stress situation, as evidenced by reduced self-reported anxiety responses in comparison to placebo and no treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can cause drowsiness and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or before driving or operating heavy machinery. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming Valeriana officinalis teas with other sedatives or medications that can enhance its sedative effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 1-2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried roots per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2-3 teaspoons as needed.
Use tea for 1-3 months to manage chronic stress and anxiety.
Consult a healthcare professional before long-term usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been widely used to treat stress and anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to reduce feelings of tension and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a sedative effect and can help to reduce stress levels by regulating the body's response to stress. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Lavandula angustifolia teas can help to alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety, leading to improved sleep quality and overall well-being. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat stress include reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression, improved mood, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the flowers. Add honey to taste if desired.
Drink the tea when it's still warm, ideally 1-2 times a day, to help manage stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Lavandula angustifolia teas for stress relief exhibit sedative properties, which help alleviate symptoms of mental stress and insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be beneficial in treating stress-related ailments, but its frequent consumption may lead to side effects such as drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its calming and sedative properties. Precautions should be taken when using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat stress, including avoiding its use before driving or operating heavy machinery, and being cautious when combining it with other medications or substances that can cause drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times daily.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Start with a small dose and adjust as needed to manage stress.
Combine with relaxation techniques like meditation or deep breathing.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as passionflower tea, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety-related ailments. The herbal preparation's properties, such as its sedative and anxiolytic effects, help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, work synergistically to exert a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. By drinking Passiflora incarnata teas, individuals can experience benefits such as improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for stress relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to steep 1 tablespoon of the flowers for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for stress relief, ideally after meals or before bedtime.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "TheScientificWorldJournal", Passiflora incarnata teas for stress have been widely used to treat anxiety since ancient times, with P. incarnata being the most studied and used species in natural anxiolytic herbal medicine formulations.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be an effective herbal remedy for stress, but it may cause drowsiness, dizziness, and gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, which can be particularly concerning when driving or operating heavy machinery. To use Passiflora incarnata teas safely, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase it as needed, and also to be aware of any potential interactions with other medications or substances, such as sedatives or antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons (5-10 ml) per cup as a general dosage.
Use tea for 7-14 days to experience stress relief benefits.
Combine Passiflora incarnata tea with meditation or yoga for better results.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been used for centuries to treat stress and anxiety-related ailments. The calming properties of this herbal preparation are attributed to its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation by slowing down the heart rate and promoting sleep. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, in Melissa officinalis teas help to reduce stress and anxiety by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas has been shown to provide benefits in reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood, and enhancing overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Remove the water from heat and add the Melissa officinalis leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC complementary medicine and therapies", Melissa officinalis teas may help alleviate stress by reducing depression and anxiety severity in patients with type 2 diabetes with depressive symptoms, as shown in a 12-week double-blind clinical trial where the extract significantly decreased depression and anxiety scores compared to the baseline.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat stress and anxiety, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using these teas include starting with small doses, avoiding them before surgery or driving, and being cautious when combining with other medications or substances, as they may interact and exacerbate side effects.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a day for stress relief.
Start with low dose (1 teaspoon) and adjust to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Use Melissa officinalis tea for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable results.
Combine with other stress-reducing activities for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties. The herbal preparation contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and avenanthramides, which contribute to its stress-relieving effects by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels. These bioactive constituents help to calm the nervous system, reduce inflammation, and promote a sense of well-being, making Avena sativa teas a popular natural remedy for stress relief. The benefits of using Avena sativa teas to treat stress include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety levels, and a sense of calm and relaxation, making it an effective herbal preparation for managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa root or 2 teaspoons of Avena sativa powder.
Choose a heat-resistant cup or teapot and pour the boiling water over the Avena sativa root or powder.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. If using powder, you can also let it steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the Avena sativa root or powder.
Drink the tea immediately, ideally 1-2 cups per day, to help with stress relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be a helpful natural remedy for stress relief, but they may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and increased heart rate in some individuals. To minimize potential issues, it's essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, as consuming excessive amounts can lead to interactions with medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Avena sativa tea for stress relief.
Take 1 teaspoon of Avena sativa powder in 1 cup of hot water, 2 times a day.
Drink the tea 30 minutes after waking up and before bedtime.
Use Avena sativa tea for 2-4 weeks, then take a 2-week break.
Adjust the dosage to 1/2 teaspoon if you experience any discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. Drinking ginger tea can help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety, and promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation. The benefits of ginger tea in treating stress and anxiety include improved mood, reduced anxiety levels, and enhanced sleep quality, making it a popular natural remedy for stress relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil in a pot or kettle.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the tea and serve hot. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas may be beneficial for stress due to its high antioxidant activity and significant presence of total phenolic compounds, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polyphenols, which can contribute to its traditional use in folkloric medicine for stress and other ailments.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy to alleviate stress, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to ginger. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with small doses, avoid mixing with other medications or herbs that may interact with ginger, and to drink the tea after meals to reduce discomfort.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea daily, ideally in the morning.
Take 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder as needed, up to 3 times a day.
Start with low doses and gradually increase as your body adjusts to the herb.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 1-2 weeks to manage stress and anxiety effectively.
Combine with a healthy lifestyle, including regular exercise and a balanced diet.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its calming and soothing effects, help to reduce stress by promoting relaxation and tranquility. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, including L-theanine, flavonoids, and polyphenols, work together to regulate the body's response to stress, reducing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calmness. By consuming Camellia sinensis teas, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced anxiety, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for stress relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white tea) and 1 cup of water.
Heat water to 200°F (boiling point) in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of tea leaves for every 8 oz of water and place them in a tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea for 1-3 minutes, depending on the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea and serve hot, adding honey or lemon to taste if needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie", Camellia sinensis teas for stress may be beneficial due to their potential anti-depressant properties, which are attributed to their rich content of phytochemical compounds such as flavanoids, steroids, saponins, and alkaloids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can help alleviate stress due to their high antioxidant content, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, or dizziness, particularly if consumed in excess. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase the amount over time, also avoiding consumption before bedtime due to its stimulating properties.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea daily for stress relief.
Choose a suitable tea type, like Green or White Camellia sinensis.
Take 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves per 8 oz of hot water.
Use 2-3 times a day for moderate stress or as needed.
Consult a doctor if stress persists or worsens despite tea use.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety-related ailments due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, have been shown to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which helps to alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety. The benefits of consuming Hypericum perforatum teas include reduced stress levels, improved mood, and enhanced sleep quality, making it a popular herbal remedy for those seeking natural stress relief. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Hypericum perforatum teas provide a safe and natural alternative for managing stress-related ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the flowers from the liquid.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Hypericum perforatum teas for stress may be most effective when consumed 2 hours before meals to provide optimal secondary health benefits associated with inhibition of postprandial stress.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat stress, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Hypericum perforatum teas safely, it's recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming them before surgery or with sedatives, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery, as they can cause drowsiness and affect cognitive function.
preparation_usage: Take 1-2 teaspoons of Hypericum perforatum tea in a cup twice daily.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before meals for better absorption.
Start with a low dose of 500mg and adjust as needed up to 2000mg.
Use Hypericum perforatum tea for 2-4 weeks to manage stress effectively.
Monitor your stress levels and adjust your dosage or frequency as needed.

The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its stress-relieving effects. These compounds help to reduce cortisol levels, promote relaxation, and improve mood, ultimately alleviating symptoms of stress and anxiety. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been shown to provide benefits such as improved sleep quality, reduced muscle tension, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup or mug.
Place the dried root in the cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature to help with stress.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat stress-related ailments, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, sodium retention, and potassium deficiency. When using these teas to alleviate stress, precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, which can lead to symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and muscle weakness.
preparation_usage: Start with 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, 2-3 times daily.
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals or as needed for stress relief.
Begin with 1/2 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup as needed and tolerance allows.
Use Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for 1-2 weeks, then reassess stress levels and adjust usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been widely used to treat stress-related ailments due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote balance. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in ginkgo biloba teas, such as quercetin and bilobalide, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to calm the mind and body. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, ginkgo biloba teas help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being. Regular consumption of ginkgo biloba teas has been shown to improve mood, reduce stress levels, and enhance overall mental health, making it a popular natural remedy for stress relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 1-2 times a day to help manage stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mechanisms of ageing and development", Ginkgo biloba teas for stress may help reduce the negative influence of stress by inhibiting monoamine oxidase (MAO) activity, particularly in older individuals.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate stress symptoms, but potential side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties, especially when taken in high doses or combined with other medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take it in moderation, avoid consuming it before surgery or with anticoagulant medications, and be aware of its potential interactions with other herbal supplements or medications.
preparation_usage: Take 1/2 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba powder in a cup of warm water.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally in the morning and afternoon.
Start with a low dose of 250mg and gradually increase to 500mg.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 4-6 weeks to see noticeable stress relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and usage.

This herbal preparation helps to treat stress by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and regulating the body's response to stress, ultimately leading to improved overall well-being. The benefits of Matricaria chamomilla teas in treating stress include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and depression symptoms, and enhanced mood stability, making them an effective and natural alternative to conventional stress-relief methods.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be beneficial in treating stress, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or difficulty breathing, especially in individuals with known allergies to plants in the daisy family. preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Matricaria chamomilla tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.
Consume 1-2 cups a day for 1-2 weeks to manage stress.

preparations.herb_desc: Humulus lupulus teas, also known as hop teas, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety due to their calming properties. The sedative effects of hop teas are attributed to their bioactive constituents, including alpha-bisabolol, humulene, and lupulone, which interact with the brain's GABA receptors to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. By regulating the body's response to stress, hop teas help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote a sense of calm, making them a beneficial herbal preparation for individuals struggling with stress-related ailments. Regular consumption of hop teas has been shown to provide a range of benefits, including improved sleep quality, reduced inflammation, and enhanced mood, making it an effective natural remedy for stress treatment.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Humulus lupulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for stress relief, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other sedative substances. To use Humulus lupulus teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid mixing it with other medications or substances that cause drowsiness, and to avoid driving or operating heavy machinery after consumption.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas, derived from the plant commonly known as gotu kola, have been used for centuries to treat stress-related ailments due to their adaptogenic and anti-anxiety properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat stress by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving mood, allowing individuals to better cope with the pressures of daily life. The bioactive constituents of Centella asiatica teas, including triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to stress relief. By consuming Centella asiatica teas, individuals can benefit from reduced stress levels, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for managing stress and promoting relaxation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Centella asiatica leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Centella asiatica leaves and add to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Centella asiatica leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the Centella asiatica tea hot or cold, as desired, to help reduce stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, Centella asiatica teas for stress may have anxiolytic activity in humans, as indicated by the attenuation of the acoustic startle response amplitude 30 and 60 minutes after treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be beneficial in treating stress-related ailments, but may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. Precautions should be taken when using Centella asiatica teas, including starting with low doses and gradually increasing as needed, avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and being cautious when combining with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, derived from the artichoke plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate stress and anxiety. The calming properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, thereby treating the ailment effectively. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin and caffeic acid, in Cynara scolymus teas possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to its stress-relieving effects. Regular consumption of Cynara scolymus teas offers numerous benefits, including reduced cortisol levels, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Cynara scolymus (artichoke) leaves, flowers, or stems.
Combine the artichoke parts with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and stir well to combine.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to help manage stress and promote relaxation.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to alleviate stress, but consuming it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its high levels of saponins and other compounds. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as starting with low doses, avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and not consuming it in excess, as this may exacerbate digestive issues and interact with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Rhodiola rosea teas have been traditionally used to treat stress-related ailments due to their adaptogenic and anti-stress properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat stress by enhancing the body's ability to cope with physical and mental stress, promoting relaxation, and improving mood. Rhodiola rosea teas are rich in bioactive constituents such as rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, which help to reduce cortisol levels, improve sleep quality, and boost mental performance. The benefits of using Rhodiola rosea teas to treat stress include improved mood, reduced fatigue, enhanced cognitive function, and a sense of overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of Rhodiola rosea dried flowers.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat and add the Rhodiola rosea dried flowers.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the Rhodiola rosea tea once or twice a day, as needed, to help manage stress.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Rhodiola rosea teas for stress may be effective in reducing stress due to its pronounced stress-protective properties and ability to modulate neurotransmission, although the study specifically examined the effects of Rhodiola extract on rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rhodiola rosea teas can be beneficial in reducing stress levels, but possible side effects include mild anxiety, insomnia, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use Rhodiola rosea teas safely, it's essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and be cautious when consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate the uterus and cause other complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper methysticum teas, also known as kava tea, have been traditionally used to treat stress and anxiety. The properties of this herbal preparation help to induce relaxation and reduce stress levels by promoting a sense of calmness and tranquility. The bioactive constituents of kava tea, including kavalactones and flavokavains, have been shown to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain to produce a sedative effect, thereby helping to alleviate stress and anxiety symptoms. The benefits of kava tea in treating stress and anxiety include reduced muscle tension, improved mood, and enhanced sleep quality, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking a non-pharmacological approach to managing stress.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup to steep.
Add the dried root to the cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Piper methysticum teas for stress have been found to be associated with anxiolytic activity in humans, suggesting potential benefits for alleviating anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper methysticum teas can be used to treat stress, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid using it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and also be aware of potential interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis teas, also known as Baikal skullcap or Huang-qin, have been traditionally used to treat stress-related ailments due to their adaptogenic and calming properties. The bioactive constituents, including baicalein and baicalin, in these teas help to reduce stress and anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and hormones in the brain, promoting relaxation and improving mood. By regulating the body's response to stress, Scutellaria baicalensis teas help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, leading to improved overall well-being and reduced risk of chronic stress-related disorders. The benefits of consuming Scutellaria baicalensis teas for stress relief include reduced cortisol levels, enhanced cognitive function, and improved sleep quality, making it a popular natural remedy for stress management.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Scutellaria baicalensis dried root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried root to a teapot or cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea and enjoy, or store it in the fridge for later use.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Biochimica et biophysica acta, Scutellaria baicalensis teas for stress may be beneficial due to their antioxidant properties, particularly baicalein, which effectively scavenged free radicals and protected cells against oxidative injury.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Scutellaria baicalensis teas can be used to treat stress and anxiety due to its adaptogenic properties, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use these teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, as excessive consumption may lead to liver damage and interactions with certain medications, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using them altogether.

preparation_study: According to Menopause (New York, N.Y.), Valeriana officinalis teas for stress do not appear to be useful in reducing depression or anxiety in this population.
intro_study: According to "Recent patents on biotechnology", teas for stress management can be effectively managed by introducing plants such as chamomile, lavender, and valerian, which are known to boost mental health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
